_comment: TASK-7.7 5b-2b-ii(0): canonical AgentBootRelay (0x41) boot-relay REQUEST frame golden vector. Freezes the enclave->host-relay->anchor wire format so the host relay daemon (5b-2b-ii(b)) and any external/anchor reimplementation match it byte-for-byte. The companion .frame.bin holds the full encoded frame; the Rust test boot_relay_golden_vector_is_byte_exact_and_round_trips asserts encode==.frame.bin AND decode(.frame.bin)==these inputs AND the canonical byte layout below.

provenance.note: This is a RUST-ENCODER-EMITTED golden: the directory's Elixir generator gen_agent_vectors.exs emits only RLP/protobuf/EIP-191 and has no CBOR or SHA3-512, so it cannot produce this frame. The byte-exact assert is therefore against the encoder that produced it; the value of the vector is (a) freezing against future encoder drift and (b) the independent canonical-layout assertions + this hand-auditable breakdown, which let an external reimplementer reproduce the bytes WITHOUT running the Rust encoder.

inputs.quote_report: opaque frame-format filler: 100 bytes of 0xA5 (NOT a valid SNP attestation; the quote does NOT embed report_data — this vector pins the FRAME only, never feed it to an SNP verifier)

derived.report_data_construction: SHA3-512( b"2d-hsm-agent-anchor-handshake-v1" || chain_id.to_be_bytes()[8] || (env.len() as u32).to_be_bytes()[4] || env.as_bytes() || nonce[32] )

frame_layout.body: canonical integer-keyed CBOR map of 7 pairs (RFC 8949 shortest-form, ascending integer keys); decoder is lenient on key ORDER but the encoder + this vector are canonical

keys.7: bytes cert_chain (VCEK->ASK->ARK, opaque; may be empty)
